catalog.rationale: The Vivid branch answered cinematographers who wanted a more decisive negative inside the ETERNA system.

reference.relationship: Independent interpretive emulation; researched, not measured

dossier.tagline: Firmer blacks, stronger chroma, and clean controlled-light texture.
dossier.portrait: ETERNA Vivid 160T is modeled around firmer blacks, stronger chroma, and clean controlled-light texture. The Vivid branch answered cinematographers who wanted a more decisive negative inside the ETERNA system. Grainlab treats that identity as a linked capture, emulsion, process, and output system—not as a color cast pasted over a digital file.

facts.label: CANON RANK
facts.note: Curatorial significance rank; not a sales chart

chapters.title: Why ETERNA Vivid 160T belongs in the century canon
chapters.lede: The Vivid branch answered cinematographers who wanted a more decisive negative inside the ETERNA system.

details.label: Material
details.value: ECN-2 motion negative; 3200 K tungsten; nominal speed / EI reference 160.

details.label: Image structure
details.value: tabular crystal interpretation, multilayer emulsion mapping, fine presentation-scale grain.

notes: The canon balances cultural use, technical influence, geographic/manufacturer breadth, process diversity, and surviving documentation; rank is curatorial rather than a claimed global sales measurement.

chapters.title: Tone, color, and material signature
chapters.lede: The target is firmer blacks, stronger chroma, and clean controlled-light texture.

details.label: Curve
details.value: Toe 0.12 · development gamma 0.97 · shoulder 0.43 · scan contrast .92.

details.label: Color / spectral posture
details.value: greenVivid channel sensitivity and density-dependent crossover; 3200 K tungsten reference balance.

details.label: Grain
details.value: fine correlated dye-cloud density variation; tabular geometry; multilayer architecture.

details.label: Halation
details.value: 3% thresholded scene-highlight scatter before the film curve; intentionally stock/process dependent.

notes: Grain is signal-dependent and spatially correlated across the full frame; it is not independent RGB noise or a repeating overlay.

chapters.title: From DSLR or iPhone to a plausible negative
chapters.lede: Begin from a low-contrast neutral SDR rendering. Undo phone HDR halos and aggressive sharpening; preserve highlight detail so the negative-style shoulder and downstream scan contrast can do the shaping.

details.label: Input normalization
details.value: Prefer RAW developed neutrally when available. For HEIC/JPEG, choose the least processed rendition; avoid baked Vivid styles, portrait relighting, clarity, and denoise.

details.label: Dynamic range
details.value: Map HDR/Display-P3 into a stable SDR working image without clipping, preserving highlight and shadow separation before the film response.

details.label: White balance
details.value: Neutralize obvious camera casts first, then let the 3200 K tungsten stock balance and emulsion sensitivity create the intended response.

details.label: Hard limit
details.value: A rendered DSLR/iPhone file already contains sensor spectral response, computational tone mapping, demosaic, white balance, and gamut decisions; this profile cannot reverse information those stages discarded.

notes: Apple ProRAW and camera RAW still need a deliberate neutral development; RAW is not automatically scene-linear or aesthetically neutral after a converter renders it.

chapters.title: A stage-by-stage rendering target
chapters.lede: sRGB decode → +0.08 EV scene placement → 0.980/1.050/1.010 channel sensitivity → toe 0.12, γ 0.97, shoulder 0.43 → ECN-2 motion negative chemistry → 3% thresholded halation → fine dye-cloud structure

details.label: Scene
details.value: Decode to linear light, apply +0.08 EV and the stock sensitivity before tone shaping.

details.label: Emulsion
details.value: Build the toe, midtone gamma, shoulder, crossover, saturation compression, and correlated image structure as one response.

details.label: Lab / optics
details.value: Model ECN-2 motion negative, chemical fog, local density contrast, and highlight scatter before the output transform.

details.label: Scan / print
details.value: Return a low-contrast negative-style scan with open highlights; the profile's output stage supplies a restrained viewing transform, not a baked blockbuster grade.

notes: All parameters are bounded artistic approximations derived from published characteristics and process knowledge, not digitized manufacturer sensitometric or spectral measurements.

bestFor: Subjects that benefit from firmer blacks, stronger chroma, and clean controlled-light texture.
Narrative sequences, mixed exposure, and highlights intended for a grading pass.
watchFor: A rendered DSLR/iPhone file already contains sensor spectral response, computational tone mapping, demosaic, white balance, and gamut decisions; this profile cannot reverse information those stages discarded.
Stacking a strong camera picture style, computational HDR, or sharpening underneath the emulation.
Judging grain at 200% instead of at 100% and intended output size.
fieldNotes: Expose for the material idea: firmer blacks, stronger chroma, and clean controlled-light texture.
Try the profile on neutral daylight, mixed light, skin, foliage, and a flat wall before deciding what its name means.

sources.title: Negative and reversal film data-sheet archive

dossier.disclaimer: ETERNA Vivid 160T is an independent Grainlab artistic emulation informed by cited public literature and historical material. It is not endorsed by Fujifilm, is not a measured ICC/LUT profile, and cannot reproduce a specific batch, lens, exposure, chemistry, printer, scanner, paper, aging state, or wavelengths absent from the digital source.
